name: task-tracker
description: Build a Task Tracker REST API with Express and SQLite. Supports creating, reading, updating, and deleting tasks with filtering, pagination, and sorting.

milestones.name: Express server + SQLite schema
milestones.description: Create an Express server listening on port 3460 with a SQLite database (better-sqlite3). Create a 'tasks' table with columns: id (integer primary key autoincrement), title (text not null), description (text default ''), status (text default 'pending', one of 'pending','in_progress','done'), priority (integer default 0, range 0-3 where 0=low,3=critical), createdAt (text, ISO 8601), updatedAt (text, ISO 8601). Implement GET /health returning {status:'ok', dbReady:true}. Export the Express app as named export 'app' from src/index.ts.
learningObjectives: Health endpoint with DB readiness check
acceptanceCriteria: SQLite database file exists after server starts
tasks table exists with correct columns

milestones.name: Task CRUD operations
milestones.description: Implement full CRUD for tasks: POST /tasks creates a task (requires title, optional description/status/priority), returns 201 with the created task including auto-generated id and timestamps. GET /tasks returns array of all tasks. GET /tasks/:id returns single task or 404. PUT /tasks/:id updates task fields (partial update), updates updatedAt, returns updated task or 404. DELETE /tasks/:id removes task, returns 204 or 404. Validate: title required and non-empty on POST, status must be valid enum, priority must be 0-3.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /tasks with {title} creates task and returns 201
GET /tasks returns array of all tasks
PUT /tasks/:id updates fields and returns updated task

milestones.name: Filtering, pagination, and sorting
milestones.description: Add query parameter support to GET /tasks: filter by status (?status=pending), filter by priority (?priority=2), filter by minimum priority (?minPriority=1), search title by substring (?search=deploy, case-insensitive). Add pagination: ?page=1&limit=10 (default page=1, limit=20). Response format becomes {tasks:[...], pagination:{page,limit,total,totalPages}}. Add sorting: ?sort=createdAt_asc, ?sort=priority_desc, ?sort=title_asc (default: createdAt_desc). Multiple filters combine with AND logic.
